decisions.briefing: Markets drifted slightly higher today in a quiet, low-conviction session — both NASDAQ and S&P 500 added just 0.2%. Portfolio is up a strong 31.6% overall and all six positions remain intact. Cash sits at $477.14, just below the $500 threshold needed to open new positions, so no buys are actionable today without first trimming a current holding. The top screener candidates (MRNA, ASTS, FDS, BROS) are worth watching for entry if cash rises above threshold from a stop-loss or profit-target trigger.

market_education.summary_en: Today was a classic low-volatility holding pattern, with equities inching up 0.2% across major indices as no major macro catalysts drove directional conviction. Markets appear to be consolidating recent gains ahead of upcoming economic data, with institutional players largely sitting on their hands. The absence of meaningful volume or sector rotation suggests market participants are in a wait-and-see mode heading into the next catalyst.
market_education.summary_zh: 今日市場呈現低波動整固格局，主要指數小幅上漲約0.2%，市場缺乏明確的宏觀催化劑驅動方向性走勢。股市似乎正在醞釀等待下一個重要數據的出現，機構投資者普遍按兵不動。成交量清淡、板塊輪動不明顯，顯示市場正在觀望，等待下一個市場觸發因素。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Cash drag refers to the performance penalty a portfolio suffers when idle cash sits uninvested. While cash provides flexibility, it earns near-zero returns compared to invested positions. Today's situation — holding $477 just below the $500 buy threshold — is a small but real example: those funds are not compounding alongside the rest of the portfolio.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「現金拖累」是指投資組合因持有未投入市場的閒置現金而導致的績效損失。雖然保留現金能提高靈活性，但其回報率幾乎為零，遠低於已投資部位的增值潛力。今日的情況正是一個小型案例——477美元的現金剛好低於500美元的建倉門檻，這些資金無法跟隨投資組合其餘部位一同複利增長。
